opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was just watching TV and suddenly my heart felt like a machine gun, my chest felt tight, and I got incredibly dizzy.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A sudden, very fast, regular racing heartbeat that started at rest about 45 minutes ago, with chest tightness and dizziness.

symptom_narrative.onset: abruptly, while sitting watching TV, like a switch being flipped
symptom_narrative.duration: this episode started about 45 minutes ago
symptom_narrative.character: a very fast, regular, machine-gun-like pounding in the chest, with a prominent pounding sensation in the neck
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: terrifying and distressing, with chest tightness, dizziness and near-fainting on standing
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: racing/pounding in the chest, and a pounding sensation in the neck
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: sudden onset at rest, switching on instantly
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up makes him very lightheaded (near-faint); walking to the kitchen brings on mild breathlessness
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: lightheadedness/near-syncope on standing, mild breathlessness on walking, profound anxiety and mild sweating, and a pounding sensation in the neck; denies crushing central chest pain and denies fainting
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: does not report prior episodes on presentation
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: sitting rigidly on the sofa because standing brings on near-fainting; alert and able to converse

family_history_as_reported.narrative: no family history of sudden cardiac death

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; has had two double espressos today; no illicit drug use

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened by the sudden sustained racing heart while alone

communication_style.typical_message_style: anxious but coherent, describing the abrupt onset clearly

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-counted pulse during the episode; described as regular and extremely fast
